commit.message:
fix(ci): disable elevation requests when GH graph built without elevation

In CI the GraphHopper graph is built using config-ci.yml which sets
graph.elevation.provider: none (skips SRTM tile downloads for faster
graph builds). Requesting elevation=true against a non-elevation graph
causes GH to return HTTP 400 'Elevation not supported!', which fails
all routing tests.

AppHost now sets GRAPHHOPPER__ELEVATIONENABLED=false on the API when
GRAPHHOPPER_PREBUILT_IMAGE is set (i.e. CI mode). Program.cs reads this
setting and passes it through to BuildRouteRequest so all route and
export endpoints respect it.

commit.message:
Fix iOS 26.4+ restore: defer handleRestore to allow pending transactions to arrive (#286)

* Initial plan

* Fix iOS restore: defer handleRestore to allow pending transactions to arrive

On iOS 26.4+, restoreCompletedTransactionsFinished may be called before
all restored transactions are delivered via updatedTransactions. This
caused handleRestore to be called with an empty array.

Fix by deferring the handleRestore delivery using a 500ms timer, giving
pending updatedTransactions callbacks time to process restored
transactions first.

commit.message:
Fix Intel Mac crash: handle arch-specific DLLs in universal binary (#144)

* Fix tight loop when xcdevice is not found on Intel Mac

When xcdevice was not found (e.g., xcode-select pointing to Command Line
Tools instead of Xcode.app), RunXcdeviceObserveAsync returned normally
causing AppleWatchLoopAsync to immediately retry in a tight loop. This
produced 165+ warnings in ~4 seconds and could exhaust resources.

Fix: throw FileNotFoundException when xcdevice is missing, and catch it
in the loop to break out entirely since retrying won't help until the
app is restarted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle arch-specific managed DLLs in universal macOS binary

dotnet publish produces managed assemblies with ReadyToRun native code
compiled for the target architecture. When creating the universal binary,
the CI was copying the arm64 app as the base, so all DLLs in MonoBundle
were arm64-specific. The x64 CoreCLR runtime rejected these with
ERROR_BAD_FORMAT (0x8007000b), causing the app to crash on Intel Macs
with 'Failed to initialize the VM'.

The fix detects architecture-specific DLLs by reading the PE header
machine field (0x14c = IL-only, anything else = arch-specific) and moves
them into .xamarin/<RID>/ subdirectories. The macOS bootstrapper already
adds both MonoBundle/ and .xamarin/<RID>/ to the Trusted Platform
Assemblies list, so each architecture now loads its own compatible
assemblies.

Fixes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Incorporate improvements from PR #143

- Add 'build-artifacts' label trigger for PR builds on all publish jobs
- Add pull_request types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]
- Track lipo failures with ::warning:: instead of silent || true
- Copy x64-only dylibs not present in arm64 build
- Merge deps.json from both architectures for complete runtime resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Skip first snapshot cycle to avoid partial captures

When joining the snapshot channel mid-stream (e.g. from a PCAP),
the first cycle may be incomplete. Standard UMDF recovery requires
waiting for a cycle boundary before processing.

Now tracks _snapshotBoundaryFound: the first SequenceVersion change
marks the boundary. Only the SECOND complete cycle (boundary to
boundary) is used for recovery. This also applies to Recovery state.

books after full DRV replay.

perf: reduce memory usage and speed up board exports (#174)

* perf: reduce memory usage and speed up board exports

Addresses repeated OOM kills on lingolinq-staging and slow board copy/
export operations. Combines infrastructure tuning with targeted code-
level optimizations identified during memory investigation.

Render config (render.yaml):
- Set WEB_CONCURRENCY=2 on web service (was unset, defaulting to 3
  Puma workers consuming ~950MB at idle on the 2GB standard plan).
- Add RUBY_YJIT_ENABLE=1 to web and worker for 15-30% Ruby speedup.
- Add jemalloc (LD_PRELOAD + MALLOC_CONF) to worker service - was
  only set on web, leaving worker with glibc malloc fragmentation.

Code optimizations:
- Add oj gem with Oj.mimic_JSON to replace stdlib JSON parser. 5-10x
  faster JSON parsing across the app, including BoardDownstreamButtonSet
  blobs and board content that get parsed repeatedly during exports.
- BoardContent.load_content: add request/job-scoped Thread.current
  cache to avoid re-parsing the same board content multiple times
  within a single request or job.
- WordData.inflection_locations_for: add request/job-scoped cache
  for inflection lookups to avoid redundant DB queries.
- ApplicationController + Worker: clear request-scoped caches after
  each request/job to prevent data leaking between contexts.
- Converters::LingoLinq.to_external: load translations once per
  board instead of once per button (was an N+1 inside a loop).
- Converters::LingoLinq.to_external_nested: deduplicate images and
  sounds early to keep array sizes manageable across many boards.

Co-authored-by: MelissaOneil <97707350+MelissaOneil@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace recursive board copy with Clowne-based orchestrator (#178)

Replace the ~500-line recursive copy_board_links_batch / relink_board_batch_for
pattern in Relinking with a two-phase linear approach:

Phase 1 (BoardCloner): Clowne-based per-board cloner handles attribute copying,
vocabulary protection, shallow clone bookkeeping, and BoardContent offload reuse.
Board#copy_for now delegates to BoardCloner.call().

Phase 2 (BoardSetCopier): Linear orchestrator replaces the recursive accumulator
pattern. Copies all downstream boards in one pass using the existing copy_for shim,
builds a global mapper hash (old_id -> new_id), then relinks all copies to point
to each other in a single sweep using the mapper. Eliminates:
- Recursive find_batches_by_global_id re-fetches per batch
- Per-replacement DB lookups (now O(1) hash lookups)
- Unbounded pending_replacements accumulator growth

Both copy_board_links_for and replace_board_for now delegate to BoardSetCopier,
preserving their public API signatures.

Specs: 75 relinking_spec examples pass (0 failures), plus 13 board_spec copy
examples and 4 boards_controller copy examples. 7 specs that mocked the removed
internal relink_board_for method were updated to verify the mapper output instead.

Net: -433 lines from relinking.rb, +130 in board_cloner.rb, +220 in
board_set_copier.rb.

commit.message:
Surface actual parse error in fmt fallback warning instead of blaming PIVOT (#39)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fix the hardcoded PIVOT blame message in `jarify fmt` when formatting
fails for unrelated reasons.

Previously, any `ParseError` that wasn't handled by the PIVOT+ORDER BY
workaround produced this confusing output:

```
could not parse SQL (formatting skipped): unsupported syntax — PIVOT with ORDER BY is a known limitation tracked in https://github.com/amfaro/jarify/issues/2
```

Now the actual `ParseError` message is surfaced, so users see the real
reason formatting was skipped (e.g. `[]::filter[]` inside a CASE
expression).

**Change:** `src/jarify/formatter.py` — capture `ParseError as exc` and
use `f"could not parse SQL (formatting skipped): {exc}"`.

**Test added:**
`test_parse_failure_warning_includes_actual_error_not_pivot_message`
asserts `"PIVOT"` and `"issues/2"` are absent from non-PIVOT failure
warnings.

Resolves #35
